header_sub: The full process — from registration to seeing your name on the 2027 map.
hero_desc_1: Do you know your local waters well? We want experts like you to build the world's first collaborative submarine map. Starting with the Catalan coast.
hero_desc_2: Every point we receive will be cross-checked against a representative sample of all submissions, ensuring the rigour and quality of each contribution to the map.
hero_note:
Registrations open June 2026.
Sign up now and we'll let you know.
what_p1: A Manamar Contributor is someone with real knowledge of the relief and zones of interest along their coast — who wants that knowledge to become part of the map.
what_p2: This is not mass crowdsourcing. It's a data layer built by people who know their area like no one else: fishermen, divers, photographers, biologists, guides.
what_p3: Every point you contribute will carry your name. Every zone you know will have a place on the map.
what_diff_1: Local knowledge can't be downloaded. It's built with years in the water. That's exactly what we're looking for.
what_diff_2: That's why the Contributors programme exists. So that the 2027 map is reliable from day one.
profile_1_desc: With years of dives in the same area and deep knowledge of the seabed.
profile_2_desc: Experts in seabeds, currents and spots that no official map records.
profile_3_desc: Scientific precision and verified data on ecosystems and biodiversity.
profile_4_desc: Routes, safe zones and points of interest practised every season.
profile_5_desc: Quality visual documentation that enriches every point on the map.
cta_desc: Registrations open June 2026. Leave your details now and you'll be among the first to receive the link to submit your points.
proceso_intro: Full roadmap from opening registrations to the launch of the collaborative map.
tl_phase1_desc: The Contributors programme is published. Any marine geography expert can register by filling in the Google Form.
tl_phase15_title: Install the app and start saving points
tl_phase15_desc: Throughout the summer season, use ManaMar on your dives. Save points of interest in your area. In September you will send them all at once.
tl_phase2_desc: All registered contributors receive an exclusive link by email to upload their GPS points, with export instructions.
tl_phase3_desc: Contributors have exactly 1 week to upload their ZIP with GPS points and photos. The system automatically groups duplicates within 50m.
tl_phase4_desc: The team manually reviews orphan points. Data quality is validated and the map base is prepared.
tl_phase5_desc: The collaborative map is published with all verified points. Each point appears linked to the contributor name.
tl_reward_desc: All contributors who submit their points during the official window will receive a free Manamar subscription for 2027.
benefits_intro: Being a contributor is not just about providing data. It's about being part of the team building the world's first professional-quality collaborative submarine map.
benefit_1_desc: Full access to the ManaMar app throughout 2027, with all features unlocked.
benefit_2_desc: Verified public profile with "Official Manamar Contributor" tag. Visibility within the community.
benefit_3_desc: Every point you contribute appears linked to your name or centre. Organic visibility with every query.
benefit_4_desc: You will receive all programme news before anyone else, straight to your email.
benefit_5_desc: Commission or discount when you recommend subscriptions to your community.
req_intro_1: You don't need to be on the team or have thousands of followers. You just need real knowledge of the seabed and the desire to share it.
req_intro_2: Local knowledge acts as a quality filter — not to exclude, but to ensure the map is reliable.
req_1_sub: Active professional or demonstrable years of experience in the zone.
req_2_sub: With coordinates, depth and at least one photo per point.
req_3_sub: Data is transferred to Manamar for the collaborative map.
req_4_sub: 1 week in September 2026. Multiple submissions allowed.
delivery_intro: A structured ZIP with a CSV of points and their photos. We give you the template — just fill it in.
delivery_1_desc: Available in the submission form. Open it in Excel, Numbers or Google Sheets.
delivery_2_desc: At least one photo per point. JPG or PNG. Named as foto_001.jpg, etc.
delivery_3_desc: You can send several ZIPs during the week. No need to do it all at once.
delivery_4_desc: The system groups duplicate points within 50m. No extra work for you.
final_desc: The programme starts September 2026. Sign up now and we'll let you know as soon as official registration opens.
final_cta: I want to be a contributor
